bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no need to make costly concessions, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid while making no-cost accommodation where possible. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility overall despite their aggressive message, but they selected issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, both of which are acceptable to me, so I keep those compatible choices while holding firm on higher-value issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent has repeated the same low-utility-to-me bid and used aggressive language, so I treat the message as pressure rather than evidence of real movement; I concede only on the lower-weight issueF while holding firm on my high-weight priorities.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small visible concession rather than moving toward the opponent’s very low-utility offers. The opponent’s offers have not improved for me and actually worsened at t=0.04, so I treat the aggressive messages as cheap talk rather than evidence that I should concede heavily. I am preserving my high-weight priorities while conceding on issueA and issueF enough to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 rather than matching the opponent’s much lower offers. The opponent’s actual bids have not improved for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but they consistently signal interest in valueC on issueF, so I concede there while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their aggressive messages are cheap talk, but their bidding suggests issueD as valueA/valueB and issueE as valueA are acceptable, while I can concede low-weight issueF to valueC. This avoids repeating my exact prior bid while preserving my most important gains on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession from my prior offer rather than matching the opponent’s low-utility proposals. The opponent has held mostly firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF and their messages are aggressive but not backed by meaningful concessions, so I will concede moderately on issueC while holding my strongest positions on issueB, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.84 while still making visible concessions on lower/less costly points such as issueF and partially on issueC. The opponent’s offers remain quite unfavorable on issueB, issueC, and issueF, though their latest offer improved issueA and keeps issueD/issueE acceptable, so I am matching some of that signal without giving up my high-weight core issues too far this early.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, making a modest concession from my prior bid while it is still early at t=0.14. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their actual offers show some movement on issueA and issueD while they remain firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF; I am matching their signaled values on issueA, issueD, issueE, and issueF while making a small move on issueB without dropping to their worst-case demand.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, since it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no need to concede down toward the opponent’s low-utility bids yet. The opponent’s actual offers show some concession on issueA and continued agreement on issueE, while they remain firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF; their aggressive messages are cheap talk, so I am responding to the bid pattern rather than the rhetoric. I keep strong value on the high-weight issues, concede issueF, and switch issueD to a value they have previously offered while preserving full utility for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 because it is early in the negotiation (t=0.18), so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, while their actual offers show only modest and inconsistent movement; however, they have signaled acceptance pressure around issueC=valueB, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC, so I keep those while holding firmer on higher-value issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.84, since at t=0.20 there is still no need to concede down to the opponent’s low-utility bids. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat on issueA and issueD but remain consistently poor for me on issueB, issueC, and issueF; their aggressive messages are cheap talk, so I am responding to the bidding pattern rather than the tone. I concede on issueC and issueF while holding firm on the high-value issueB and keeping mutually plausible choices on issueA, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong but no-longer-maximal utility of about 0.824, conceding on lower-cost or already-tested points while holding firm on high-value issueB and avoiding issueD:valueC because it is costly to me. At t=0.22 there is still time, so I should not chase the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their bids show they strongly push issueB/issueF toward valueC and have recently offered issueC:valueB, while their aggressive “lowball” messages are cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

I am targeting a utility around 0.80, making a modest concession from my last bid while staying well above the low-utility proposals the opponent has been sending. At t=0.24 there is still time, and the opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics; their actual bids show they strongly favor issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC while accepting issueE=valueA and sometimes issueD=valueA, so I concede on lower-cost/contested areas without giving up my core value entirely.

At t=0.26 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 while making a meaningful concession on issueB and issueF, which the opponent has consistently pushed toward valueC. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their bids show they can accept issueA=valueA in some cases and just offered issueC=valueA, so this bid moves toward their revealed pattern without accepting their low-utility package outright.

I am targeting about 0.76 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my ideal while still protecting high-weight issues where I can. At t=0.28 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s latest offer, but their bids show they strongly prefer issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, so I concede those while holding issueC=valueA. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, so I rely on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting about 0.76 utility, which is still strong for me but includes meaningful concessions because we are in the early-mid phase rather than at the deadline. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueA/issueB/issueF toward their side while keeping issueE at valueA, and their latest move to issueB:valueB suggests that value may be a viable compromise. Their repeated “lowball” messages are cheap talk, so I’m responding to the actual bid pattern by conceding issueA and issueF while holding higher-value positions on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting about 0.76 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my strongest bids while still preserving value on high-weight issues. At t=0.32 there is pressure to move toward the opponent, but it is not late enough to accept their low-utility offers outright; their actual offers suggest they strongly prefer issueF: valueC and often push issueA/issueC downward, while issueE: valueA is mutually acceptable. I am therefore conceding issueF and issueA, keeping issueC at my top value, and matching their latest issueD preference without cost to me.

I am targeting a high but plausible utility around 0.92: the opponent’s latest offer is already strong for me on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE, but still asks me to take the worst outcome on the important issueB and on low-weight issueF. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I will concede issueF to their repeated preference and keep the mutually plausible issueB: valueB, which they have offered before, while ignoring the aggressive “lowball” messaging and focusing on bid behavior.

I targeted a solid mid-game utility around 0.84, conceding on issueC from my ideal while holding the high-value gains the opponent just signaled on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest valid offer improved substantially for me, so I am making a reciprocal compromise rather than reverting to my prior exact bid. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but the offer history shows they may accept issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA, with the main gap now on issueC and issueF.

At t=0.38 I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility around 0.76 rather than staying near my maximum, since we are entering the mid-game but not yet near the deadline. The opponent’s actual bids, despite aggressive lowball language, show they consistently insist on issueE:valueA and issueF:valueC, and their latest offer gives me issueA:valueA and issueC:valueA while pushing issueB and issueD down; I will concede on issueD and issueF while holding issueB at a high-value compromise.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 while making meaningful concessions on lower-cost or repeatedly requested positions, especially issueF: valueC and moderate values on issueA/issueC. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their bids show some real movement toward valueB on issueA/issueC/D and occasional willingness on issueB: valueB, so I am holding issueB at valueB rather than conceding to valueC yet.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.824 while still making meaningful concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueF, especially since issueF is lower weight for me and the opponent consistently insists on valueC there. At t=0.42 there is pressure to move toward bids the opponent has signaled, but not enough to collapse to their latest low-utility offer; their messages are mostly pressure, while their actual offers show some willingness around issueA/valueB, issueB/valueB, issueC/valueB, and issueE/valueA.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.76, conceding on issueA and issueF while holding stronger positions on issueC and issueD. The opponent’s offers and messages show persistent pressure, but the actual bidding suggests they strongly favor issueF: valueC and issueE: valueA, and their last offer also accepts issueB: valueB and issueD: valueA. Since time is 0.44, I will move closer to their latest bid without accepting the lower-utility parts wholesale.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility of about 0.76 rather than accepting the opponent’s latest much lower-value offer. At t=0.46 there is some time pressure to make a credible move, so I am returning to a package the opponent previously proposed, while conceding on issueF and issueC but holding stronger positions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE. The opponent’s messages are aggressive but cheap talk; the offer history is more useful, and this bid is based on an actually signaled acceptable structure.
